entries.summary: New York hush money criminal trial against Trump opened with jury selection.
Trump's New York trial resumed for third week with more witness testimony.
Day 10 of trial included testimony anchoring election-conspiracy framing by prosecutors.
Jurors heard closing arguments as the New York hush money trial neared end.
New York jury found Trump guilty on all 34 felony falsification counts.
Trump became first US ex-president convicted, publicly rejecting the New York trial.
Supreme Court ruled presidents have partial immunity for official acts in office.
Trump survived an assassination attempt at a rally in Butler, Pennsylvania.
Federal judge dismissed Trump's classified documents prosecution two days after Butler attack.
Republican National Convention formally nominated Trump for a second presidential term.
President Biden withdrew from race and endorsed Vice President Kamala Harris.
Trump and Harris met in their sole presidential debate of the 2024 race.
Trump survived a second assassination attempt at his Florida golf club.
Trump's New York sentencing was delayed past the November presidential election.
Trump defeated Harris to win the United States presidential election.
Congress certified Trump's victory four years after January 6 Capitol attack.
New York judge sentenced Trump to unconditional discharge, sparing him any penalty.
Jury ordered Trump to pay E. Jean Carroll $83.3m for defamation.
CNN reported Trump juggling four criminal cases alongside Republican primary campaign.
CNN analyzed Trump's tangled trial calendar against the political primary schedule.
Analysis examined what trying a former US president would mean historically.
CNN published comprehensive explainer on New York hush money trial mechanics.
Trump's first criminal trial opened in Manhattan, an unprecedented historical moment.
Trump tried to blend court appearances with campaign rallies between sessions.
Trump held Wisconsin and Michigan rallies on a rare court off-day.
CNN tracked Trump's tightening weekly schedule between trial and battleground rallies.
Manhattan jury convicted Trump on all 34 counts of falsifying business records.
Supreme Court granted presidents broad immunity for official acts in office.
CNN analyzed how Butler attack and Vance pick transformed the 2024 campaign.
Harris and Trump debated in Philadelphia, their first and only meeting.
Trump won presidential election, sweeping all seven battleground states.
Trump transition team began naming cabinet picks shortly after election victory.
Judge Merchan sentenced Trump to unconditional discharge in the New York case.
Trump was inaugurated as the 47th US president, returning to office.
